entry.title: Re: Concern about "Inscriptions"
entry.summary:
The efficiency and security implications of various cryptographic key schemes highlight significant challenges. In the realm of ECDSA (Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm), the intentional reuse of `k` values not only compromises security but also permits the encoding of additional data within both the `k` value itself and the secret key. This method reveals that both can be computed, which poses a significant risk. Moreover, attackers have been known to forcefully decode or "bruteforce" a `k` value to implant data in signatures, a technique evident in certain compromised hardware key exfiltration scenarios.
Furthermore, the possibility extends to encoding data within public keys by bruteforcing these keys as well. Such practices underscore the inherent difficulties in curtailing the storage of arbitrary data within systems where security measures are based on the unpredictability and secrecy of key data. This approach, while theoretically sound, leads to impracticalities and high costs when implemented, especially in environments where data integrity and security are paramount. The exploration of these aspects sheds light on the delicate balance between operational efficiency and robust security protocols in cryptographic practices.
